caveat: This framework content pack is a structured starter representation and does not constitute legal advice or complete regulatory coverage. This is a starter content pack for scoping purposes only. It does not constitute legal advice or a complete compliance determination.

obligations.title: Apply GDPR processing principles
obligations.description: Personal data processing shall follow lawfulness, fairness, transparency, purpose limitation, minimization, accuracy, storage limitation, integrity, and accountability principles.
obligations.applicability_notes: Applies to all personal data processing activities.

obligations.title: Document lawful basis per processing purpose
obligations.description: Identify and document lawful basis for each processing purpose under GDPR Article 6.
obligations.plain_language_summary: Keep lawful basis records for each processing purpose.
obligations.applicability_notes: Maintain lawful basis mapping in data inventory/ROPA artifacts.

obligations.title: Provide transparent privacy notices
obligations.description: Provide clear privacy notices describing processing purposes and rights.
obligations.applicability_notes: Notice content should be role- and channel-appropriate.

obligations.title: Support right of access and portability workflows
obligations.description: Implement and operate request workflows for GDPR access and portability rights.
obligations.plain_language_summary: Operate GDPR access and portability workflows.
obligations.applicability_notes: Include response and exception handling procedures.

obligations.title: Support rectification and erasure rights
obligations.description: Implement workflows to support correction and deletion of personal data.
obligations.applicability_notes: Include policy on legal hold and exemptions.

obligations.title: Support objection and restriction rights
obligations.description: Handle objections/restriction requests and update processing controls accordingly.
obligations.applicability_notes: Applies where legal basis permits objections.

obligations.title: Maintain records of processing activities
obligations.description: Maintain current records of processing activities as required by GDPR Article 30.
obligations.applicability_notes: Include categories, recipients, retention, and transfer details.

obligations.title: Implement security of processing controls
obligations.description: Implement technical and organizational measures appropriate to risk under Article 32.
obligations.applicability_notes: Encryption, resilience, restoration, and testing should be addressed.

obligations.title: Execute data processing agreements
obligations.description: Execute and maintain processor contracts with required GDPR clauses.
obligations.applicability_notes: Applies where processors/sub-processors handle personal data.

obligations.title: Perform DPIAs for high-risk processing
obligations.description: Perform DPIAs where processing is likely to result in high risk.
obligations.applicability_notes: Consult supervisory authority where residual high risk remains.

obligations.title: Notify supervisory authority of qualifying breaches
obligations.description: Notify personal data breaches to supervisory authorities within required timelines where applicable.

obligations.title: Notify affected data subjects for high-risk breaches
obligations.description: Communicate breach impacts to data subjects when rights and freedoms are likely at high risk.
obligations.applicability_notes: Must include plain-language risk and mitigation details.

obligations.title: Designate DPO where required
obligations.description: Appoint a Data Protection Officer where legal thresholds are met and ensure role independence.
obligations.applicability_notes: Track DPO responsibilities and reporting lines.

obligations.title: Use valid transfer mechanisms
obligations.description: Apply lawful mechanisms for international transfers of personal data and maintain supporting assessments.
obligations.applicability_notes: Includes SCCs, adequacy decisions, and transfer impact assessment controls.

obligations.title: Maintain accountability evidence
obligations.description: Maintain evidence demonstrating GDPR compliance, including policies, logs, training, and governance decisions.

content_versions.obligation_text: Apply core GDPR processing principles consistently across processing activities.
Document lawful basis for each processing purpose under Article 6.
Provide transparent privacy notices with mandatory disclosures.
Operate data subject access request workflows within legal timelines.
Implement rectification and erasure handling workflows.
Process objections and restrictions with documented outcomes.
Maintain records of processing activities and updates.
Implement Article 32 security of processing measures.
Execute and maintain data processing agreements with required clauses.
Conduct DPIAs for high-risk processing and track actions.
Notify supervisory authority of qualifying breaches within deadlines.
Notify affected data subjects for high-risk breach scenarios.
Designate and support a DPO where legal criteria are met.
Use valid data transfer mechanisms and maintain transfer safeguards.
Maintain accountability evidence to demonstrate compliance posture.
